claim: In the days following the Charlie Kirk shooting, a viral social-media thread identified an unnamed man wearing a brown shirt and sunglasses in the audience footage and proposed that he was the actual operator — either of a remote detonator or of a concealed 'palm gun.' The man has been variously identified as Rick Cutler in independent videos. The FBI's own person-of-interest images, released on September 11, 2025, showed a different individual wearing a dark blue cap and a black T-shirt with an American flag and bald eagle, photographed on a campus stairwell. That person was subsequently identified as Tyler Robinson; the 'brown shirt' man named in the viral thread has not been named in any official law-enforcement statement on the case.

sources.label: FBI — Utah Valley Shooting Updates (person-of-interest photos, Sep 11 2025)
CNBC — “Charlie Kirk shooting: FBI shares person of interest photos, offers $100,000 reward”
Axios — “Police release photo of person of interest in Charlie Kirk shooting death”
sources.quote: The photos show a person wearing sunglasses, a dark baseball cap, a dark long-sleeved shirt, dark pants and sneakers with white laces.

sources.label: YouTube — “Charlie Kirk Conspiracy: Following Rick Cutler, The Palm Gun guy. (Was not a detonator)”
BIBLE Companion / @bible.app_, Instagram reel DTQb5oiAMHb (Jan 8, 2026) — surfacing source

notes: The 'brown shirt' / 'palm gun' figure is the subject of a substantial TikTok and YouTube ecosystem of speculation, but the only formal law-enforcement person-of-interest images released on September 11, 2025 depict a different individual (later identified as Tyler Robinson). The YouTube debunk linked above traces the brown-shirt man back to an attendee named Rick Cutler and argues the hand gesture interpreted as a detonator-press is not a detonator. This record documents both layers — the viral identification and the official record — and leaves the inference to the reader.
